name: Blackened Catfish
tagline: Cajun-spiced catfish seared to a crackly crust in cast iron
highlight: Smoking-hot pan, dry spice — no shellfish, all crust

hero.alt: Blackened catfish with charred Cajun crust
hero.caption: blackened catfish — cajun crust

healthFacts: Catfish is firm, mild, and budget-friendly — takes spice well.
Smoke point of butter is low; that's why blackening is messy and ventilation matters.

steps.title: Mix the blackening spice
steps.detail: Combine all dried spices in a small bowl.
steps.tip: Make a triple batch and keep in a jar — works on chicken, shrimp, anything.

steps.title: Coat the fillets
steps.detail: Brush both sides of fish with melted butter. Press into spice mix to coat thickly.
steps.tip: Thick coating = real crust. Don't skimp.

steps.title: Screaming hot pan
steps.detail: Heat cast iron over high until smoking. Lay fish in (no extra fat). Cook 3 min per side until spice is jet-black and fish flakes.
steps.tip: Open a window. Turn on the fan. This is going to smoke.

steps.title: Serve immediately
steps.detail: Squeeze of lemon, side of rice, side of greens. Eat now — blackening is best fresh.
steps.tip: Serve with a cool yogurt or remoulade to balance the heat.
